chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

text: 749 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ سَأَلَهُ: عَنْ جِلْدِ اَلْمَيْتَةِ يُلْبَسُ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ إِذَا دُبِغَ فَقَالَ «لاَ وَ إِنْ دُبِغَ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.749 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that he asked him about wearing the skin of a dead animal (carrion) during prayer if it has been tanned.
Imam (as) said: "No, even if it has been tanned seventy times."
